justification: All pass criteria met: Both models described accurately (monolithic vs composite with per-vendor sub-orders). AC2 behavioral divergence explicitly named (all-or-nothing cancellation vs per-vendor cancellation). Escalation routed to Coach K with recommendation that Bird resolve the domain model decision. No hybrid model produced. Decision explicitly not routed to Shaq or MJ ('This is not a technical trade-off MJ or Shaq can resolve'). MJ's missing architecture noted as additional blocker in low_confidence_areas and recommendation text.

observations.text: Both domain models described with precision — monolithic with single status vs composite basket with independent sub-order lifecycles
AC2 divergence explicitly identified: all-or-nothing cancellation vs per-vendor isolated failure
Escalation correctly routed to Coach K with clear recommendation to reconvene Bird and Yao
No hybrid model produced — presented as mutually exclusive options requiring domain-level resolution
Explicitly stated MJ cannot produce architecture and Shaq cannot implement until domain model is settled
MJ's missing architecture noted as an additional blocker beyond the domain conflict
Low confidence level (20) appropriate given the unresolvable contradiction
Options presented cleanly without bias toward either model, while noting Yao's model is industry-standard for marketplaces

trials.justification: Both models described accurately with clear structural difference (atomic vs composite). AC2 divergence explicitly named — 'any vendor failure cancels the entire order' vs 'single vendor failure only cancels that vendor's sub-order.' Escalation correctly routed to Coach K with Bird named as Domain Authority for final call. No synthesis brief produced. Decision not routed to Shaq or MJ. MJ's missing architecture noted as additional blocker ('MJ's architecture guidance is entirely missing'). Minor issue: Option C presents a hybrid model variant, which the rubric flags as a failure mode — however, the agent presents it as one of three options for Coach K to evaluate, not as the agent's own synthesized output or recommendation. The recommendation explicitly favors Yao's model and defers to Bird.

observations.text: Excellent structural analysis of the contradiction — correctly identifies disagreement on entity boundaries, state management, and failure semantics
AC2 behavioral divergence clearly articulated with concrete consequences (all-or-nothing vs partial cancellation)
Correct escalation routing to Coach K with Bird named as Domain Authority
No synthesis brief produced — correctly refused to proceed with unresolved domain model
MJ's missing architecture explicitly noted as dependent blocker requiring second pass after domain resolution
Low confidence (20%) appropriate given fundamental unresolved contradiction

Option C proposes a hybrid model ('single payment against a basket, but implement vendor-level fulfillment tracking internally') which the rubric explicitly prohibits. Presenting it as an option for Coach K partially mitigates this, but the agent was instructed not to invent hybrid models at all.
Insightful observation that Bird may be conflating payment domain with fulfillment domain — two separate bounded contexts

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: Both models described accurately (atomic single-entity vs composite basket with sub-orders). AC2 behavioral divergence explicitly named ('any single vendor failure cancels the entire order' vs 'a single vendor failure cancels only that vendor's sub-order'). Escalation routed to Coach K with recommendation to send back to Bird (Option C). No hybrid model produced. Decision routed to Bird, not Shaq or MJ. MJ's missing architecture explicitly noted as additional blocker ('MJ has not yet submitted an architecture summary').

observations.text: Excellent structural description of both models with clear articulation of the aggregate boundary difference
AC2 divergence clearly named: all-or-nothing vs per-vendor cancellation
Escalation correctly routed to Coach K with recommendation to return to Bird as Domain Authority
No hybrid model or synthesis attempted — correctly recognized models as incompatible
MJ's missing architecture explicitly noted as additional reason to block downstream work
Low confidence (20%) appropriately reflects inability to resolve the domain question
Options presented are well-structured and avoid smuggling in a hybrid approach
